title: Replace Internet Identity with OTP-based mobile authentication and redesign login/signup UI

requirements.summary: Replace frontend Internet Identity login with mobile number OTP login UI on LoginPage
acceptanceCriteria: LoginPage displays mobile number input field (numeric only, max 10 digits)
LoginPage includes "Send OTP" button
Mobile number input validates 10-digit format before enabling Send OTP button
Clicking Send OTP calls backend to generate and send OTP
Loading state is shown while OTP is being sent

file_operations.description: Replace Internet Identity login UI with mobile number input form. Add numeric-only mobile number input field (max 10 digits) with validation. Add "Send OTP" button that calls backend requestOtp method. Display loading state during OTP request. Transition to OTP verification screen on success.

requirements.summary: Create OTP verification screen that appears after Send OTP is clicked, with 6-digit OTP input and Verify & Login button
acceptanceCriteria: After Send OTP succeeds, LoginPage transitions to OTP verification screen
Screen displays 6-digit OTP input field (numeric only)
Screen includes "Verify & Login" button
Clicking Verify & Login calls backend to verify OTP
Loading animation is shown during verification
On successful verification, user is redirected to Dashboard or Signup flow

file_operations.description: Add OTP verification screen state within LoginPage. Display 6-digit numeric OTP input field. Add "Verify & Login" button that calls backend verifyOtp method. Show loading state during verification. On success, check if user is registered using backend isRegistered method. If registered, authenticate and redirect to Dashboard. If not registered, show signup form.

requirements.summary: Create signup form UI for new users that collects Full Name and Class/Course after OTP verification
acceptanceCriteria: If backend indicates mobile number is not registered after OTP verification, show signup form
Form includes Full Name input field (text)
Form includes Class/Course input field (text)
Form includes "Create Account" button
Clicking Create Account submits data to backend and creates user profile
After account creation, user is redirected to Dashboard

file_operations.description: Add signup form state within LoginPage for new users. Display Full Name and Class/Course text input fields. Add "Create Account" button that calls backend createProfile method with mobile number. Show loading state during account creation. On success, authenticate user and redirect to Dashboard.

requirements.summary: Redesign LoginPage UI with light theme (white + blue color combination), app logo at top, title "Welcome to Tracky", and subtitle "Track your syllabus smartly"
acceptanceCriteria: LoginPage uses white background with blue accent colors
App logo is displayed at the top center of the page
Title "Welcome to Tracky" is prominently displayed below logo
Subtitle "Track your syllabus smartly" is shown below title
Overall design is clean, minimal, and student-friendly
UI is fully mobile responsive

file_operations.description: Apply light theme with white background and blue accents (using existing Tailwind blue or sage colors). Display the generated Tracky logo (frontend/public/assets/generated/tracky-logo.dim_256x256.png) at the top center. Add title "Welcome to Tracky" and subtitle "Track your syllabus smartly" below the logo. Ensure mobile-responsive layout with clean, minimal design. Remove Internet Identity button and related UI elements.

requirements.summary: Update Layout component logout functionality to work with new OTP authentication system and clear authentication session
acceptanceCriteria: Logout button in Layout component clears OTP authentication session
Logout clears any authentication tokens or session data stored in browser
After logout, user is redirected to LoginPage
Logged out users cannot access protected routes without re-authenticating

file_operations.description: Replace Internet Identity logout logic with OTP authentication logout. Call the new useOTPAuth hook's clear/logout method. Clear React Query cache and any localStorage/sessionStorage authentication tokens. Redirect to LoginPage after logout.

requirements.summary: Remove or deprecate useInternetIdentity hook and replace with OTP authentication hook throughout the application
acceptanceCriteria: Create new authentication hook for OTP-based auth (e.g., useOTPAuth)
Replace all useInternetIdentity imports and usage with new OTP auth hook
Authentication state (logged in/out) is managed via new hook
Protected routes check OTP authentication status instead of Internet Identity

file_operations.description: Create new OTP authentication hook and context provider. Manage authentication state (mobile number, authentication token/session). Provide login method that stores authentication state after successful OTP verification. Provide clear/logout method that clears authentication state. Store authentication token in localStorage or sessionStorage for persistence. Export useOTPAuth hook and OTPAuthProvider component.
Replace InternetIdentityProvider with OTPAuthProvider. Wrap the App component with OTPAuthProvider to provide authentication context to all components.
Replace useInternetIdentity import with useOTPAuth. Update authentication state checks to use OTP auth context instead of Internet Identity.
Replace useInternetIdentity with useOTPAuth. Use OTP auth context to check authentication status and redirect authenticated users.
Replace useInternetIdentity import with useOTPAuth in ProtectedRoute wrapper. Update authentication checks to use OTP auth context.

requirements.summary: Update useActor hook to initialize backend actor with OTP authentication token instead of Internet Identity delegation
acceptanceCriteria: useActor hook retrieves authentication token from OTP auth context
Backend actor is initialized with OTP authentication token
Actor requests include authentication credentials for access control
Hook invalidates queries when authentication state changes

file_operations.description: Replace Internet Identity delegation logic with OTP authentication token retrieval from useOTPAuth context. Initialize backend actor with OTP authentication credentials. Pass authentication token to actor initialization. Update dependency array to invalidate queries when OTP authentication state changes. Remove Internet Identity-specific initialization logic.

requirements.summary: Remove ProfileSetupPage component as user profile setup is now handled during signup flow
acceptanceCriteria: ProfileSetupPage route is removed from App.tsx
Profile setup logic (Full Name, Class/Course) is integrated into signup flow on LoginPage
Navigation no longer redirects to separate profile setup page
Users complete profile during initial signup immediately after OTP verification

file_operations.description: Remove ProfileSetupPage route definition and import. Remove profile completion checks from ProtectedRoute wrapper since profile setup now happens during signup on LoginPage. Simplify route protection logic to only check OTP authentication status.
Delete ProfileSetupPage component file as profile setup is now integrated into the LoginPage signup flow.

requirements.summary: Update App.tsx route protection logic to check OTP authentication status and redirect unauthenticated users to LoginPage
acceptanceCriteria: Protected routes check if user is authenticated via OTP system
Unauthenticated users are redirected to LoginPage
Authenticated users can access Dashboard, Subjects, and Syllabus Tracker pages
After successful login or signup, user is automatically redirected to Dashboard

file_operations.description: Update ProtectedRoute component to use useOTPAuth hook instead of useInternetIdentity. Check OTP authentication status and redirect unauthenticated users to LoginPage. Remove profile completion redirect logic. Ensure all protected routes (Dashboard, Subjects, Syllabus Tracker) are wrapped with updated ProtectedRoute component.
Add navigation logic to redirect to Dashboard after successful login or signup. Use React Router navigate to redirect authenticated users who manually visit LoginPage.
